_comment: Path overrides applied only when a tool is dispatched against an on-prem
ESET PROTECT On-Prem and ESET Connect (cloud) share the vast majority of
REST paths, but a few diverge — usually because on-prem keeps a legacy
action verb. Each entry below names the exact cloud (service, method,
path) tuple and the on-prem replacement to use.
If a tool name is not listed here, the cloud path is used verbatim and
the on-prem console either accepts it or returns 404 (which the agent
sees as a plain ESET API error).
